Question:
We plan to conduct a weighted logistic regression analysis to examine the relationship between serum albumin (g/dl) and diabetic retinopathy using data from the years 2011 to 2020. 
The study will include all individuals whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older who have complete data on both serum albumin (g/dL) and diabetic retinopathy status.
Participants will be excluded if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old, or age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30, or had uncertain diabetes status. 
Uncertainty is based on their answer to the question “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” and if they answer as "borderline" and  either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%. 
We will also exclude anyone with missing data on serum albumin or on the outcome measure.
The main predictor in the analysis will be serum albumin (g/dL), categorized into quartiles Q1: <25%, Q2: ≥25 and <50, Q3: ≥50 and <75%, Q4: ≥75%. 
The outcome variable will be the presence or absence of diabetic retinopathy, based on responses to the question “diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?”, coded as 1 for "Yes" and 0 for "No".
We will adjust the analysis for potential confounding factors including gender, age in years at screening, and race/Hispanic origin, which will be categorized as White, Black, Mexican American, or Other, 
We will also adjust for kidney disease status, which is defined as havinig an estimated glomerular filtration rate (eGFR) below 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 or urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) of 30 mg/g or greater. It will be categorized as yes, no, and unknown.  
eGFR is defined for 4 cases:
if gender is female and serum_creatinine <= 0.7 (mg/dl): 142 * (serum_creatinine(mg/dl) / 0.7)**(-0.241) * (0.9938)**age at screening * 1.012
if gender is female and serum_creatinine > 0.7 (mg/dl): 142 * (serum_creatinine(mg/dl) / 0.7)**(-1.2) * (0.9938)**age at screening* 1.012
if gender is male and serum_creatinine <= 0.9 (mg/dl): 142 * (serum_creatinine (mg/dl)/ 0.9)**(-0.302) * (0.9938)**age at screening
if gender is male and serum_creatinine > 0.9 (mg/dl) :142 * (serum_creatinine(mg/dl) / 0.9)**(-1.2) * (0.9938)**age at screening
Structured Question:

Dataset: NHANES

Period of Interest: 2011-2020

- age in years at screening >= 30

Exclusion Criteria:
- age when first told the patient had diabetes < 30
- “Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?” = "borderline" AND (glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) is missing OR glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) < 6.5%)
- missing serum albumin (g/dL)
- missing diabetic retinopathy status

Covariates:
- gender
- age in years at screening
- race/Hispanic origin categorized as:
  White,
  Black,
  Mexican American,
  Other
- kidney disease status categorized as:
  yes,
  no,
  unknown
  defined as:
    eGFR (mL/min/1.73m^2) calculated as:
      If gender is female and serum_creatinine <= 0.7 (mg/dl):
        eGFR = 142 * (serum_creatinine(mg/dl) / 0.7)**(-0.241) * (0.9938)**age at screening * 1.012
      If gender is female and serum_creatinine > 0.7 (mg/dl):
        eGFR = 142 * (serum_creatinine(mg/dl) / 0.7)**(-1.2) * (0.9938)**age at screening * 1.012
      If gender is male and serum_creatinine <= 0.9 (mg/dl):

        eGFR = 142 * (serum_creatinine(mg/dl) / 0.9)**(-1.2) * (0.9938)**age at screening

    Kidney disease status = "no" if eGFR >= 60 mL/min/1.73m^2 AND urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR) < 30 mg/g

SQL:
-- STEP 1: Create temp_master_weight

-- TEMP TABLE FOR weight
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_weight;

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_weight AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight * 2.0 / 9.2) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_g
WHERE full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight * 2.0 / 9.2) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_h
WHERE full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight * 2.0 / 9.2) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.demo_i
WHERE full_sample_2_year_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       (full_sample_mec_exam_weight * 3.2 / 9.2) AS survey_weight
FROM nhanes.p_demo
WHERE full_sample_mec_exam_weight IS NOT NULL;


-- STEP 2: Create temp_psu_stratum

-- TEMP TABLE FOR PSU and Stratum
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_psu_stratum;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_psu_stratum AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.demo_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.demo_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.demo_i
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, 
       masked_variance_pseudo_psu AS masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
       masked_variance_pseudo_stratum AS masked_variance_pseudo_stratum
FROM nhanes.p_demo;


-- STEP 3: Create final weight+design table
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS weight_design;
CREATE TABLE weight_design AS
SELECT 
    s.respondent_sequence_number,
    s.masked_variance_pseudo_psu,
    s.masked_variance_pseudo_stratum,
    w.survey_weight
FROM temp_psu_stratum s
INNER JOIN temp_master_weight w
  ON s.respondent_sequence_number = w.respondent_sequence_number;




-- TEMP TABLE FOR "age at screening", "age in years at screening", "age in years at screening"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM nhanes.p_demo
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM nhanes.demo_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM nhanes.demo_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_in_years_at_screening" AS "age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM nhanes.demo_i;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening ON temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "age when first told the patient had diabetes"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" AS "age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.p_diq
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" AS "age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" AS "age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" AS "age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_i;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes ON temp_master_age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" AS "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.p_diq
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" AS "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" AS "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" AS "doctor_told_you_have_diabetes"
FROM nhanes.diq_i;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes ON temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "glycohemoglobin (hba1c) (%)"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_glycohemoglobin;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_glycohemoglobin AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "glycohemoglobin" AS "glycohemoglobin"
FROM nhanes.p_ghb
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "glycohemoglobin" AS "glycohemoglobin"
FROM nhanes.ghb_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "glycohemoglobin" AS "glycohemoglobin"
FROM nhanes.ghb_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "glycohemoglobin" AS "glycohemoglobin"
FROM nhanes.ghb_i;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_glycohemoglobin ON temp_master_glycohemoglobin (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "serum albumin (g/dl)", "serum albumin (g/dl)", "serum albumin (g/dl)"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_albumin_g_dl;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_albumin_g_dl AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "albumin_g_dl" AS "albumin_g_dl"
FROM nhanes.p_biopro
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "albumin_g_dl" AS "albumin_g_dl"
FROM nhanes.biopro_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "albumin_g_dl" AS "albumin_g_dl"
FROM nhanes.biopro_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "albumin_g_dl" AS "albumin_g_dl"
FROM nhanes.biopro_i;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_albumin_g_dl ON temp_master_albumin_g_dl (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "diabetes affected eyes had retinopathy?", "diabetic retinopathy status", "diabetic retinopathy status"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" AS "diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy"
FROM nhanes.p_diq
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" AS "diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy"
FROM nhanes.diq_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" AS "diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy"
FROM nhanes.diq_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" AS "diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy"
FROM nhanes.diq_i;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy ON temp_master_diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "gender", "gender"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_gender;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_gender AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "gender" AS "gender"
FROM nhanes.p_demo
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "gender" AS "gender"
FROM nhanes.demo_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "gender" AS "gender"
FROM nhanes.demo_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "gender" AS "gender"
FROM nhanes.demo_i;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_gender ON temp_master_gender (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "race/hispanic origin", "race/hispanic origin"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_race_hispanic_origin;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "race_hispanic_origin" AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM nhanes.p_demo
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "race_hispanic_origin" AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM nhanes.demo_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "race_hispanic_origin" AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM nhanes.demo_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "race_hispanic_origin" AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM nhanes.demo_i;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_race_hispanic_origin ON temp_master_race_hispanic_origin (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "serum_creatinine (mg/dl)"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "creatinine_mg_dl" AS "creatinine_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.p_biopro
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "creatinine_mg_dl" AS "creatinine_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.biopro_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "creatinine_mg_dl" AS "creatinine_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.biopro_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "creatinine_mg_dl" AS "creatinine_mg_dl"
FROM nhanes.biopro_i;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl ON temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl (respondent_sequence_number);

-- TEMP TABLE FOR "urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (uacr) (mg/g)"
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_master_albumin_creatinine_ratio_mg_g;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_master_albumin_creatinine_ratio_mg_g AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "albumin_creatinine_ratio_mg_g" AS "albumin_creatinine_ratio_mg_g"
FROM nhanes.p_alb_cr
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "albumin_creatinine_ratio_mg_g" AS "albumin_creatinine_ratio_mg_g"
FROM nhanes.alb_cr_g
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "albumin_creatinine_ratio_mg_g" AS "albumin_creatinine_ratio_mg_g"
FROM nhanes.alb_cr_h
UNION ALL
SELECT respondent_sequence_number, "albumin_creatinine_ratio_mg_g" AS "albumin_creatinine_ratio_mg_g"
FROM nhanes.alb_cr_i;
-- Add index for faster joins
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_master_albumin_creatinine_ratio_mg_g ON temp_master_albumin_creatinine_ratio_mg_g (respondent_sequence_number);

-- ALL UNIQUE PATIENT IDs
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_all_ids;
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_all_ids AS
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_glycohemoglobin
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_albumin_g_dl
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_gender
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_race_hispanic_origin
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl
UNION
SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM temp_master_albumin_creatinine_ratio_mg_g;

-- Add index to the main ID table for the final join
CREATE INDEX idx_temp_all_ids_respondent_sequence_number ON temp_all_ids (respondent_sequence_number);

-- FINAL MASTER TABLE
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS final_master_table;
CREATE TABLE final_master_table AS
SELECT
    a.respondent_sequence_number,
    t0."age_in_years_at_screening",
    t1."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes",
    t2."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes",
    t3."glycohemoglobin",
    t4."albumin_g_dl",
    t5."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy",
    t6."gender",
    t7."race_hispanic_origin",
    t8."creatinine_mg_dl",
    t9."albumin_creatinine_ratio_mg_g"
FROM temp_all_ids a
LEFT JOIN temp_master_age_in_years_at_screening t0 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t0.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes t1 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t1.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_doctor_told_you_have_diabetes t2 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t2.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_glycohemoglobin t3 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t3.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_albumin_g_dl t4 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t4.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy t5 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t5.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_gender t6 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t6.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_race_hispanic_origin t7 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t7.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_creatinine_mg_dl t8 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t8.respondent_sequence_number 
LEFT JOIN temp_master_albumin_creatinine_ratio_mg_g t9 ON a.respondent_sequence_number = t9.respondent_sequence_number;

CREATE INDEX idx_final_master_respondent_sequence_number    
    ON final_master_table (respondent_sequence_number);


-- Clean up final_master_table
DELETE FROM final_master_table
WHERE respondent_sequence_number NOT IN (
    SELECT respondent_sequence_number FROM weight_design
);


-- ========== STEP 1: Inclusion ==========

-- STEP 1.1: Includes all patients whose age in years at screening was 30 years or older
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_inclusion_step1 AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM final_master_table
WHERE "age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- ========== STEP 2: Exclusions ==========

-- STEP 2.1: Exclude participants if their age in years at screening is less than 30 years old
-- (This step is redundant with inclusion but kept for explicit adherence to exclusion rules)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step1 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_inclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_in_years_at_screening" IS NULL OR f."age_in_years_at_screening" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.2: Exclude participants if age when first told the patient had diabetes was before age 30
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step2 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step1 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" IS NULL OR f."age_when_first_told_you_had_diabetes" >= 30;

-- STEP 2.3: Exclude participants with uncertain diabetes status
-- Uncertainty is based on their answer to "Has a doctor ever told you that you have diabetes?" as "borderline"
-- AND either have a missing glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) value or an glycohemoglobin (HbA1c) below 6.5%.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step3 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step2 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE NOT (f."doctor_told_you_have_diabetes" = 'Borderline' AND (f."glycohemoglobin" IS NULL OR f."glycohemoglobin" < 6.5));

-- STEP 2.4: Exclude anyone with missing data on serum albumin (g/dL)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step4 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step3 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- STEP 2.5: Exclude anyone with missing data on diabetic retinopathy status
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_exclusion_step5 AS
SELECT i."respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step4 i
JOIN final_master_table f ON i."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number"
WHERE f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" IS NOT NULL;

-- ========== STEP 3: Cohort ==========

-- The final temp table in the exclusion sequence becomes temp_cohort.
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_cohort AS
SELECT "respondent_sequence_number"
FROM temp_exclusion_step5;

-- ========== STEP 4: Variables ==========

-- 4.1 Gender
-- Extract gender for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_gender AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.2 Age in years at screening
-- Extract age in years at screening for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_age_in_years_at_screening AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.3 Race/Hispanic origin
-- Map race/hispanic origin to specified categories
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_race_hispanic_origin AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic White' THEN 'white'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Non-Hispanic Black' THEN 'black'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" = 'Mexican American' THEN 'mexican american'
           WHEN f."race_hispanic_origin" IN ('Other Hispanic', 'Other Race - Including Multi-Racial') THEN 'other'
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "race_hispanic_origin"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- 4.4 Kidney disease status
-- Calculate eGFR (mL/min/1.73m^2) based on gender, serum_creatinine, and age at screening using CKD-EPI 2021 formula
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_egfr_calculation AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."gender",
       f."creatinine_mg_dl",
       f."age_in_years_at_screening",
       f."albumin_creatinine_ratio_mg_g",
       CASE
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."creatinine_mg_dl" <= 0.7 THEN 142 * POWER((f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7), -0.241) * POWER(0.9938, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.012
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Female' AND f."creatinine_mg_dl" > 0.7 THEN 142 * POWER((f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.7), -1.2) * POWER(0.9938, f."age_in_years_at_screening") * 1.012
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."creatinine_mg_dl" <= 0.9 THEN 142 * POWER((f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9), -0.302) * POWER(0.9938, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
           WHEN f."gender" = 'Male' AND f."creatinine_mg_dl" > 0.9 THEN 142 * POWER((f."creatinine_mg_dl" / 0.9), -1.2) * POWER(0.9938, f."age_in_years_at_screening")
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Derive Kidney disease status from eGFR and urine albumin-to-creatinine ratio (UACR)
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_kidney_disease_status AS
SELECT e."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN e."egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2" IS NULL OR e."albumin_creatinine_ratio_mg_g" IS NULL THEN 'unknown'
           WHEN e."egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2" < 60 OR e."albumin_creatinine_ratio_mg_g" >= 30 THEN 'yes'
           WHEN e."egfr_ml_min_1_73m_2" >= 60 AND e."albumin_creatinine_ratio_mg_g" < 30 THEN 'no'
           ELSE 'unknown' -- Should not happen if previous conditions are exhaustive
       END AS "kidney_disease_status"
FROM temp_egfr_calculation e;

-- 4.5 Serum albumin (g/dL)
-- Extract raw serum albumin values for the cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_raw AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       f."albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- Compute quartile cutoffs for serum albumin within cohort
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs AS
SELECT
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.25) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS q1,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5)  WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS q2,
  PERCENTILE_CONT(0.75) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY "albumin_g_dl") AS q3
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw
WHERE "albumin_g_dl" IS NOT NULL;

-- Assign serum albumin quartile group
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_serum_albumin_quartiles AS
SELECT s."respondent_sequence_number",
  CASE
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" IS NULL THEN NULL
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q1 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q1: <25%'
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q2 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q2: ≥25 and <50%'
    WHEN s."albumin_g_dl" < (SELECT q3 FROM temp_serum_albumin_cutoffs) THEN 'Q3: ≥50 and <75%'
    ELSE 'Q4: ≥75%'
  END AS "serum_albumin_g_dl"
FROM temp_serum_albumin_raw s;

-- 4.6 Diabetic retinopathy status
-- Map diabetic retinopathy status to binary (1 for "Yes", 0 for "No")
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_diabetic_retinopathy_status AS
SELECT c."respondent_sequence_number",
       CASE
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'Yes' THEN 1
           WHEN f."diabetes_affected_eyes_had_retinopathy" = 'No' THEN 0
           ELSE NULL
       END AS "diabetic_retinopathy_status"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN final_master_table f ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = f."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 5: Final Table ==========
CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_table AS
SELECT
  c."respondent_sequence_number",
  g."gender",
  a."age_in_years_at_screening",
  r."race_hispanic_origin",
  k."kidney_disease_status",
  sa."serum_albumin_g_dl",
  dr."diabetic_retinopathy_status"
FROM temp_cohort c
JOIN temp_gender g ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = g."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_age_in_years_at_screening a ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = a."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_race_hispanic_origin r ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = r."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_kidney_disease_status k ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = k."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_serum_albumin_quartiles sa ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = sa."respondent_sequence_number"
JOIN temp_diabetic_retinopathy_status dr ON c."respondent_sequence_number" = dr."respondent_sequence_number";

-- ========== STEP 6: Final Output ==========
SELECT * FROM temp_final_table;

-- STEP: Create merged final table with design variables + in_analysis flag
DROP TABLE IF EXISTS temp_final_with_weights;

CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_final_with_weights AS
SELECT 
    wd."respondent_sequence_number",
    wd."masked_variance_pseudo_psu",
    wd."masked_variance_pseudo_stratum",
    wd."survey_weight",
    CASE WHEN ft."respondent_sequence_number" IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS "in_analysis",
    ft."gender",
    ft."age_in_years_at_screening",
    ft."race_hispanic_origin",
    ft."kidney_disease_status",
    ft."serum_albumin_g_dl",
    ft."diabetic_retinopathy_status"
FROM weight_design wd
LEFT JOIN temp_final_table ft
  ON wd."respondent_sequence_number" = ft."respondent_sequence_number";
  
-- Preview the result
SELECT * FROM temp_final_with_weights;

suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(dplyr))
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(broom))
    suppressPackageStartupMessages(library(tibble))

    identifier_vars <- c("respondent_sequence_number")
    outcome_vars <- c("diabetic_retinopathy_status")
    weight_vars <- c("survey_weight","masked_variance_pseudo_psu","masked_variance_pseudo_stratum","in_analysis")

    cohort <- df[df$in_analysis == 1, names(df) != "in_analysis"]


    outcome_roles <- c()
    outcome_roles["diabetic_retinopathy_status"] <- "outcome"


    summarize_variable <- function(column, name) {
    n_unique <- length(unique(na.omit(column)))
    na_count <- sum(is.na(column))

    if (name %in% identifier_vars) {
        category <- "identifier"
        summary_info <- paste0("Total rows = ", nrow(cohort))

    } else if (name %in% outcome_vars) {
        # Assign its special role (e.g., "outcome", "mediator")
        category <- ifelse(!is.na(outcome_roles[name]), outcome_roles[name], "outcome")

        # Generate a summary based on its underlying data type
        underlying_type <- case_when(
        n_unique == 2                                 ~ "binary",
        is.numeric(column) & n_unique > 2             ~ "numerical",
        is.character(column) | is.factor(column)      ~ "categorical",
        TRUE                                          ~ "other"
        )

        if (underlying_type == "numerical") {
        mean_val <- round(mean(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        sd_val   <- round(sd(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        summary_info <- paste0("mean = ", mean_val, ", sd = ", sd_val)
        } else if (underlying_type %in% c("categorical", "binary")) {
        tbl <- prop.table(table(column)) * 100
        tbl <- round(tbl, 1)
        summary_info <- paste0(names(tbl), ": ", tbl, "%", collapse = "; ")
        } else {
        summary_info <- NA
        }

    

    } else if (name %in% weight_vars) {
        category <- "weight"
        summary_info <- "Weight variable" 
    

    } else {
        # This block handles regular predictors
        category <- case_when(
        n_unique == 2                                 ~ "binary",
        is.numeric(column) & n_unique > 2             ~ "numerical",
        is.character(column) | is.factor(column)      ~ "categorical",
        TRUE                                          ~ "other"
        )

        if (category == "numerical") {
        mean_val <- round(mean(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        sd_val   <- round(sd(column, na.rm = TRUE), 3)
        summary_info <- paste0("mean = ", mean_val, ", sd = ", sd_val)
        } else if (category %in% c("categorical", "binary")) {
        tbl <- prop.table(table(column)) * 100
        tbl <- round(tbl, 1)
        summary_info <- paste0(names(tbl), ": ", tbl, "%", collapse = "; ")
        } else {
        summary_info <- NA
        }
    }

    data.frame(
        variable   = name,
        category   = category,
        n_unique   = n_unique,
        na_count   = na_count,
        summary    = summary_info,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    }

    report <- bind_rows(
    lapply(names(cohort), function(col_name) {
        summarize_variable(cohort[[col_name]], col_name)
    })
    )

    identifier_vars  <- report$variable[report$category == "identifier"]
    cat("Identifier Variables:\n");   print(identifier_vars);   cat("\n")

    weight_vars <- report$variable[report$category == "weight"]; cat("Weight Variables: "); print(weight_vars); psu_var <- weight_vars[grepl("psu", weight_vars, ignore.case = TRUE)][1]; cat("PSU:", psu_var); stratum_var <- weight_vars[grepl("stratum", weight_vars, ignore.case = TRUE)][1]; cat("Stratum:", stratum_var); weight_var <- weight_vars[grepl("weight", weight_vars, ignore.case = TRUE)][1]; cat("Weight:", weight_var); in_analysis_var <- weight_vars[grepl("in_analysis", weight_vars, ignore.case = TRUE)][1]; cat("In Analysis:", in_analysis_var)

    binary_vars      <- report$variable[report$category == "binary"]
    cat("Binary Variables:\n");       print(binary_vars);       cat("\n")
    print(binary_vars)

    categorical_vars <- report$variable[report$category == "categorical"]
    cat("Categorical Variables:\n");  print(categorical_vars);  cat("\n")
    print(categorical_vars)

    numerical_vars   <- report$variable[report$category == "numerical"]
    cat("Numerical Variables:\n");    print(numerical_vars);    cat("\n")
    print(numerical_vars)

    
    

    outcome_var     <- report$variable[report$category %in% c("outcome")]
    cat("Outcome Variables:\n");      print(outcome_var);      cat("\n")
    

    
    library(survey)
    options(survey.lonely.psu = "adjust")
        
    
    
    # Identify predictors and factor candidates
    predictor_vars <- report$variable[report$category %in% c("categorical", "binary", "numerical")]
    factor_candidates <- report$variable[report$category %in% c("categorical", "binary")]

    # Convert binary/categorical variables to factor if not already
    for (var in factor_candidates) {
    if (!is.factor(df[[var]])) {
        df[[var]] <- as.factor(df[[var]])
    }
    }

    # Initialize reference_levels table
    reference_levels <- tibble(variable = character(), reference_level = character())

    # Clean, trim, and set reference levels for all factor variables
    for (col in factor_candidates) {
    df[[col]] <- factor(trimws(as.character(df[[col]])))
    levels_vec <- levels(df[[col]])

    # Try to find preferred reference
    preferred_order <- c("healthy", "none", "control", "reference", "ref", "normal","Q1", "low", "0", "1")
    matches <- tolower(levels_vec)  # lower case to standardize
    ref_level <- NA  # default

    for (keyword in preferred_order) {
    idx <- which(grepl(keyword, matches, ignore.case = TRUE))
    if (length(idx) > 0) {
        ref_level <- levels_vec[idx[1]]  # pick the first match for that keyword
        break  # stop at the highest-priority match
    }
    }


    # If none found, fall back to most common level
    if (is.na(ref_level)) {
        level_counts <- table(df[[col]])
        ref_level <- names(level_counts)[which.max(level_counts)]
    }

    # Set reference level
    df[[col]] <- relevel(df[[col]], ref = ref_level)

    # Record reference level
    reference_levels <- bind_rows(
        reference_levels,
        tibble(variable = col, reference_level = ref_level)
    )
    }
    
        
    
    x_raw <- df[[outcome_var]]
    x_chr <- trimws(tolower(as.character(x_raw)))

    none_tokens <- c("none", "nan", "na", "", "null", "nil", "missing")
    x_chr[x_chr %in% none_tokens] <- NA_character_
    y <- rep(NA_integer_, length(x_chr))
    y[x_chr %in% c("yes", "y", "true", "t")]  <- 1L
    y[x_chr %in% c("no",  "n", "false", "f")] <- 0L
    x_num <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(x_chr))
    y[is.na(y) & !is.na(x_num) & x_num == 1] <- 1L
    y[is.na(y) & !is.na(x_num) & x_num == 0] <- 0L
    idx <- is.na(y) & !is.na(x_chr)
    if (any(idx)) {
    vals <- sort(unique(x_chr[idx]))
    if (length(vals) != 2) {
        stop(sprintf(
        "Outcome '%s' must have exactly 2 non-missing classes after excluding none. Found: %s",
        outcome_var, paste(vals, collapse = ", ")
        ))
    }
    tab <- table(x_chr[idx])
    minority <- names(tab)[which.min(tab)]
    majority <- setdiff(vals, minority)
    cat(sprintf(
        "Outcome '%s' recoding: 1 = %s (minority)  0 = %s (majority)",
        outcome_var, minority, majority
    ))
    y[idx] <- as.integer(x_chr[idx] == minority)
    }
    df[[outcome_var]] <- y
    u <- sort(unique(df[[outcome_var]][!is.na(df[[outcome_var]])]))
    if (!(length(u) == 2 && all(u == c(0, 1)))) {
    stop(sprintf(
        "After recoding, '%s' must contain both 0 and 1 (plus NA for none). Found: %s",
        outcome_var,
        ifelse(length(u) == 0, "<no non-missing values>", paste(u, collapse = ", "))
    ))
    }
    formula <- as.formula(paste0(outcome_var, " ~ ", paste(predictor_vars, collapse = " + ")))
            
    
    survey_design <- svydesign(
        ids     = as.formula(paste0("~", psu_var)),
        strata  = as.formula(paste0("~", stratum_var)),
        weights = as.formula(paste0("~", weight_var)),
        data    = df,
        nest    = TRUE
        )
    survey_design <- subset(survey_design, in_analysis ==1)
        
    
    model <- svyglm(formula,design = survey_design, family = quasibinomial()) 
            
    
    result_table <- tidy(model, conf.int = TRUE, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
            select(term, estimate, p.value, conf.low, conf.high)
